title: paspalum sanguinale var. didactylum
intro: Paspalum sanguinale var. didactylum, also known as two-toed paspalum, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Paspalum sanguinale var. didactylum include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves or roots in water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or topical creams for medicinal use.
